p-1.plaintext: Hydra separates your production frontend from the editing experience. Here's how to deploy for different scenarios.

children.text: Hydra separates your production frontend from the editing experience. Here's how to deploy for different scenarios.
SPA/Hybrid — Full Visual Editing

p-4.plaintext: The simplest setup — your frontend handles both production and editing:

children.text: The simplest setup — your frontend handles both production and editing:

ol-5.plaintext: Deploy your frontend as SPA or Hybrid (SSR + client-side hydration) Deploy Hydra and the Plone API server Login to Hydra, go to user preferences, set your frontend URL

children.text: Deploy your frontend as SPA or Hybrid (SSR + client-side hydration)
Deploy Hydra and the Plone API server
Login to Hydra, go to user preferences, set your frontend URL

p-6.plaintext: This gives you all visual editing features including inline text editing, drag and drop, and realtime preview.

children.text: This gives you all visual editing features including inline text editing, drag and drop, and realtime preview.
SSG/SSR — Production Speed + Visual Editing

p-8.plaintext: Get the speed of static generation while keeping visual editing. Deploy two versions of the same frontend:

children.text: Get the speed of static generation while keeping visual editing. Deploy two versions of the same frontend:

ol-9.plaintext: Production — Deploy your frontend in SSG or SSR mode (fast, cacheable) Editing — Deploy the same frontend in SPA mode to a separate URL (used only in Hydra) Hydra + Plone — Only needs to run during editing, so scale-to-zero / serverless works SSG rebuild — For SSG, configure collective.webhook to trigger a rebuild on edit. SSR doesn't need this.

children.text: Production
— Deploy your frontend in SSG or SSR mode (fast, cacheable)
Editing
— Deploy the same frontend in SPA mode to a separate URL (used only in Hydra)
Hydra + Plone
— Only needs to run during editing, so scale-to-zero / serverless works
SSG rebuild
— For SSG, configure
collective.webhook
to trigger a rebuild on edit. SSR doesn't need this.
Example: The Nuxt.js Demo

p-11.plaintext: The default Hydra demo uses exactly this pattern:

children.text: The default Hydra demo uses exactly this pattern:

ul-12.plaintext: Production — SSG on Netlify. All pages statically generated, images optimized, fast globally. Editing — Same Nuxt codebase deployed as SPA to a different Netlify URL. Only loaded inside Hydra's iframe. Hydra + Plone — Deployed to fly.io with scale-to-zero. Cost is free or minimal since it only runs during editing.

children.text: Production
SSG on Netlify
. All pages statically generated, images optimized, fast globally.
Editing
— Same Nuxt codebase deployed as SPA to a different Netlify URL. Only loaded inside Hydra's iframe.
Hydra + Plone
— Deployed to
fly.io
with scale-to-zero. Cost is free or minimal since it only runs during editing.

p-13.plaintext: For most frameworks switching between SSG/SSR and SPA is just a config toggle, so you get the best of both worlds with minimal effort.

children.text: For most frameworks switching between SSG/SSR and SPA is just a config toggle, so you get the best of both worlds with minimal effort.
Without Hydra (Two-Window Editing)

p-15.plaintext: You can use Plone headless without Hydra — use Volto for editing and your custom frontend for viewing.

children.text: You can use Plone headless without Hydra — use Volto for editing and your custom frontend for viewing.
To set this up:

ol-17.plaintext: Deploy the Plone API server Deploy your frontend Deploy a Volto site with a default theme Set up your content types and block types

children.text: Deploy the Plone API server
Deploy your frontend
Deploy a Volto site with a default theme
Set up your content types and block types

p-18.plaintext: - Currently adding new blocks requires a custom Volto theme to be deployed     - Content types can be added via Volto's Site Setup

children.text: - Currently adding new blocks requires a custom Volto theme to be deployed     - Content types can be added via Volto's Site Setup
During editing

ul-20.plaintext: You'll use Volto with its out-of-the-box theme, so it won't look like your frontend. Any new blocks you create will have a skeleton presentation within the Volto preview. Any header/footer CSS etc. won't reflect your frontend. Once a page is edited, editors switch to another tab and load the frontend URL to see the result.

children.text: You'll use Volto with its out-of-the-box theme, so it won't look like your frontend.
Any new blocks you create will have a skeleton presentation within the Volto preview.
Any header/footer CSS etc. won't reflect your frontend.
Once a page is edited, editors switch to another tab and load the frontend URL to see the result.

p-21.plaintext: - If the page is private, you'll additionally need a login flow on your frontend.

children.text: - If the page is private, you'll additionally need a login flow on your frontend.

h-22.plaintext: If you need a more WYSIWYG editing experience

children.text: If you need a more WYSIWYG editing experience

ul-23.plaintext: Theme Volto to recreate the design (this duplicates effort you already did on the frontend). Or just use Hydra.

children.text: Theme Volto to recreate the design (this duplicates effort you already did on the frontend).
Or just use Hydra.

description: How to deploy Hydra with SPA, SSG/SSR, or scale-to-zero setups
